methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Orchid is a Series A biotech and health company founded by Noor Siddiqui that has raised $16.5M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including peers such as Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, and New Limit.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy base, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $33M. The peer-median method draws on a 7-company cohort at the same or adjacent stage, with valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B and a median of $810M. Public-comparable anchoring cannot be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed—a common limitation for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $28M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $422M. The substantial spread reflects the wide variance in peer valuations and the stage-dependent uncertainty inherent in Series A assessment. Key uncertainties include the maturity and clinical or commercial traction of Orchid relative to its peer set, as well as the applicability of more mature Series B valuations to an earlier-stage company. The peer-median estimate of $810M should be interpreted as an upper anchor reflecting mix bias toward later-stage rounds within the comp sample.
